/motion.title: Keeo Lu
p: 2D/3D MOTION GRAPHIC DESIGNER BASED IN LONDON.

To celebrate Onitsuka Tiger’s first Barcelona store, we produced a surreal mixed-reality video blending physical and digital elements. I led the 3D production and compositing, crafting dreamlike visuals that capture the brand’s bold, experimental identity and the city’s spirit.
I created a series of videos for Yonex’s 2025 apparel line, visualising three key technical innovations:
Precision Move – Special cutting for optimal performance
Sustainability – Recycled strings transformed into new fabric blends
Very Cool – Heat-absorbing tech for a cooling effect
The main challenge was to translate these abstract technologies into bold, material-driven 3D visuals—while staying true to Yonex’s clean, performance-led brand tone.

As a motion designer with a background in fashion design, I have always been fascinated by using innovative and unique methods to better showcase garments and fabrics. I placed high-quality 3D-scanned clothing models inside a crystal box, using its sharp refraction to simultaneously display different angles of the garments. The icy impression created also echoes the setting in which puffer jackets are worn. This project is an interesting experiment in combining fashion with 3D motion.

In this project, I also attempted to clone various 3D-scanned garments and place them in different positions within a crystal box. Additionally, I experimented with changing different materials, lighting, and other factors to achieve more interesting effects.

In the early stages of this live-action compositing collaboration, we aimed to present the product in a way that better reflects the brand's identity within the display scene. I designed a series of decorative trees inspired by Art Deco, attempting to hang the product on the trees to create an immersive experience for the audience.
This project was created during the pandemic while I was interning at a creative studio.

Given the circumstances of the time, with unstable policies and lockdowns, I observed that many people were struggling with mental challenges. I, too, was affected deeply. I saw this project as an opportunity to provide support and inspiration, not only for myself but also for others, in managing and improving emotional well-being.

As a result, I identified several common mental issues as the focus of my research. I then chose food as the visual medium for this project, believing that, as living beings, we naturally find food inherently appealing and comforting. After all, simply viewing good-looking food can evoke a sense of pleasure.
I conducted research around four themes: fear, depression, obsessive-compulsive disorder, and sleep disorders, and represented each of them as a distinct dish.

During the research and development phase of this project, I had to navigate between theoretical and visual channels to find the most effective associations. I once read a quote in a book: 'Creativity does not emerge from thin air, but from the new connections formed in the brain using existing materials. This quote perfectly explains the creative process behind my work.

To enhance the 'therapeutic effect' of the project, I labeled each component and presented the story behind each food design in a more direct manner. Much like many dishes, a well-chosen name can make it easier for people to recall the dish.

For each mental health issue, I researched and discovered some interesting clinical treatments as well as a few 'folk remedies'. For example, to address phobias, people have used Exposure Therapy — gradually exposes people to their fears in a safe environment to help them overcome them. Additionally, some methods that are quite effective but often overlooked for alleviating depression include sun exposure and interacting with pets or animals.

In a world where the essence of Chinese New Year seems to fade with each passing year, especially for the younger generation, there is a growing yearning to rekindle the authentic spirit of this cherished tradition.Having conducted extensive research on Burberry, I found that Burberry's 2019 CNY campaign received poor reviews due to a misunderstanding of its target audience and cultural stereotypes. With this project, I delivered visual campaign strategy to better fit the context, combining the brand identity, cultural aesthetics and audience preferencesIn contrast to Burberry's recent Chinese New Year campaign, which simply invited celebrities to shoot a chilly "family portrait",  my design project seeks to revive the traditional Chinese New Year experience in an innovative and cinematic manner, imbued with a nostalgic touch.

Color of red, fireworks, couplets, empty streets... I've integrated these impressions, which are the most obvious representations of the Chinese New Year atmosphere, into the Burberry Chinese New Year campaign in the form of 8-bit artOn the one hand, it will resonate with more young Chinese consumers(target), and on the other hand, it will help promoting the immersive Chinese New Year culture to the world through the brand.

/3d-fashion.title: Keeo Lu
p: This project presented a significant challenge. ZITA TAN's collection primarily relies on intricate draping techniques to shape the garments, which rendered conventional methods of digitizing flat patterns into three-dimensional forms ineffective.

To accurately replicate each piece in 3D software, I conducted an on-site visit to her studio, capturing comprehensive images from multiple angles to thoroughly understand the structure and details. To achieve a highly realistic result, I meticulously refined the models by iterating through various software, applying the photographed textures to the 3D models. The final outcome not only met expectations but also enhanced my expertise in complex garment modeling.

In reality, the 8th continent is a giant rubbish dump floating in the Pacific Ocean.In this project, it is reimagined as an artificial utopia of the "water bears", in which the ubiquitous plastic waste serves as their food, while evolving them into new species.

History tends to repeat itself. Humanity discovered and invaded the paradise where a pure species lived and thrived. This pristine species was quickly tamed and turned into tools for human use. It wasn't long before humanity, driven by its innate greed and intelligence, conquered this paradise, becoming the rulers of this world and the gods who dominate other species.
2D/3D MOTION GRAPHIC DESIGNER BASED IN LONDON.

/photography.title: Keeo Lu
p: 2D/3D MOTION GRAPHIC DESIGNER BASED IN LONDON.

/tangibles.title: Keeo Lu
p: In this project, I fulfilled a long-held aspiration of mine—applying chemical experiments to design. Although I don’t intend to showcase the final results (as they are not fully refined), I’m glad I documented the joy of the process.

I conducted two crystallization experiments—copper sulfate and alum. The experiment began with acquiring the necessary materials and equipment, setting up an alcohol burner in my dorm room, controlling the concentration of the solutions, and then waiting for the crystals to form, repeating the process over and over. Once I became proficient in the crystallization process, I began introducing different materials, such as threads, mesh and artificial flowers, to attempt to influence the shape of the crystals. All in all, the experience was incredibly enjoyable.

During my design journey at Holonomic, I was fortunate to collaborate with my colleague Sisi on creating an accessory. We visited a lot of factories that specialize in making jewelry, during which we discovered some intriguing components—one of which was a metal clasp used for connecting necklaces or bracelets, composed of a ball with a button and a pin. This combination caught our attention as it reminded us of LEGO, giving consumers the freedom to be creative.

We envision each smallest unit consisting of a ball and a pin, allowing it to connect with any other unit to create new designs. We were trying to use this concept in rings, earrings, and necklaces, breaking the boundaries between different types of jewelry. They’re detachable and assemble easily, and we ensure that the new designs created are also wearable. This way, customers can mix and match to create their own unique pieces.
We sketched out the concept using abstract shapes to represent the structure and also created models to test the feasibility of the design.

Throughout the project's development, we faced numerous challenges and obstacles. For instance, when we attempted to enhance the design of this component, we struggled to find manufacturers willing to accommodate our technical innovations, and those that did often exceeded our budget constraints. However, these difficulties only reinforced our belief in the uniqueness of our idea—there was nothing like it on the market at the time. Eventually, we were fortunate enough to find a manufacturer willing to collaborate with us in developing and producing the accessory, sharing our commitment to design innovation.

Although the final product did not achieve commercial success due to various practical reasons, the experience allowed me to gain valuable insights into the jewelry design and production process. It also gave me a profound sense of satisfaction and accomplishment in bringing a design concept to life.
2D/3D MOTION GRAPHIC DESIGNER BASED IN LONDON.

/graphic.title: Keeo Lu
p: Universal Cosmo is an art exhibition that integrates fashion at a fashion showroom in Zhengzhou, China, in celebration of its opening. It features a number of national artists in its exhibitions, and the concept of Universal Cosmo is derived from the brand's diverse aesthetic and fashion products.

"Fang Yuan" (Square & Circle) is a type experiment exploring the relationship between the golden ratio and typography. In this project, I set a rule: only golden ratio-based perfect circles can be used to carve into the letterforms. The thickness, length, and structure of the characters remain flexible, allowing for future transformation and variation. To ensure legibility, I chose basic Arabic numerals as the foundation of this experiment.

As a DNA testing service firm, 23Mofang, like its counterparts, faces the biggest marketing problem - consumers greatly doubt the accuracy of its test results. Paradoxically, as the number of participants increases, the accuracy of the test will continue to rise.

Why is it that entertainment like tarot cards, which is free of scientific evidence, is more popular? To eliminate prejudice of consumers, I provided 23Mofang with a visual marketing strategy featuring Tarot and Shadow Puppets, an art form with authentic Chinese DNA.

During the summer of '23, I had the privilege of being commissioned to undertake the conceptualization of branding and product design for HD-Official, the creative brand established in 2022 by MaSiwei, an Chinese rapper from the 88rising. I maintained the brand's signature employment of aluminum bags while conjuring captivating and youthful imagery to grace various assortments within their product line.
